title: Syzygium refertum
intro: Syzygium refertum, also known as the Queensland satinash, is a plant species belonging to the family Myrtaceae, native to the subtropical regions of Australia. The health benefits of Syzygium refertum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Syzygium refertum is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used in topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
